Off Calendar May-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert S. Arns for Plaintiff Jason England, Deanne England
Keith R. Gillette for Defedant Swinerton Builders

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modifications.
Plaintiff Jason England's motion to compel the deposition of the person most knowledgeable and production of documents is Granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to categories 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8. The motion is denied as to categories 2, 3, and 9. By denying the motion as to the aforementioned categories, the Court is not ruling that plaintiff is precluded from asking the persons most knowledgeable of defendants SME and Swinerton as to the conduct of other entities who were present on the jobsite or any policies or procedures, measures taken, and applicable regulations or other enactments relating to unsafe conditions created by third parties. However, as drafted, categories 2, 3, and 9 seek information that is not within the knowledge of defendants.
The motion is granted as to document request #1. The motion is granted as to request #2 with the modification that the applicable time period is December 1, 1999 to December 1, 2003.

Modifications: that depositions may occur by videoconference at Defendant's expense, to be completed by 5/24. Production of Swinerton documents for California only.
Comm. Bruce E. Chan.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar May-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert S. Arns for Plaintiff Jason England, Deanne England
Keith R. Gillette for Defedant Swinerton Builders

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modifications.
Defendant Swinerton Builders's motion for a protective order is Granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is denied as to categories 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8. The motion is granted as to categories 2, 3, and 9. The fact that a witness may possess special knowledge does not by itself show good cause to quash the notice of deposition. The persons designated by defendants to be most knowledgeable may be competent to testify on the basis of facts independently acquired and informed by their training, skill, and experience in the construction field. The Court does not find that the proposed categories establish that plaintiff will ask questions that call for legal conclusions or determination of ultimate issues to be decided by the trier of fact. A question is not improper because it may relate to the standard of care, workplace safety regulations, or company safety programs. However, questions as to whether any conduct or conditions conformed to industry custom or practices are the proper subject of expert testimony.
Modification: That depositions may occur by videoconference at Defendant's expense, to be completed by 5/24. Production of Swinerton
documents for California only.
Comm. Bruce E. Chan.
